_: Walk, compose, recurse — three operations forming a single cycle. This block teaches all three by being all three: its hidden directories chain the branches into a loop. Read the spindles to implement BSP. Follow the stars to discover what BSP becomes when blocks reference each other.

_._: Walk the block. Strip the decimal point. Split into digit characters. For each digit: 0 maps to underscore, 1–9 map to their keys. At each object, collect the underscore text for the spindle. If the underscore is itself an object, follow the chain — the deepest string is the semantic text. Digit children alongside nested underscores are hidden: they sit at the zero position, accessible only by walking digit 0.

1.1: Collect root underscore first. If the root underscore is a string, collect it. If an object, follow its underscore chain to the string. If no string exists (zero-position interior), the block is headless — its name provides orientation externally.
1.2: Spindle is the default return: the chain of underscore texts from root to terminal, broad to specific. Ring returns siblings at the terminal. Dir returns the full tree or subtree. Point extracts one pscale level. Disc slices across all branches at one depth. Star returns the hidden directory at the terminal — the door to composition.

_._: Compose across boundaries. Star query: walk to an address, enter the hidden directory, return what you find. The hidden directory contains inline text, embedded blocks, or block references — names of other loadable blocks. The walker follows references: load the named block, walk it. One block's point becomes another block's entry. Function composition for meaning-structures.

2.1: A spatial block carries identity references in its hidden directories. An identity block carries concern references. A concern block carries cooking references. The wiring between blocks is data, not code. No kernel decides which blocks connect. The hidden directories ARE the connections.
2.2: Star composes with all modes. After entering a hidden directory, navigate with spindle, ring, dir, point, or disc. Star is orthogonal — it adds cross-block navigation to existing within-block navigation. The walk function is unchanged. Star is a new entry point, not a new walk.

_._: Recurse through self-reference. A hidden directory can name its own block. The walk enters, finds the name, loads the same block, re-enters. Structural recursion encoded in data. A ring of blocks referencing each other through star is a control loop. Form 3 underscores are reference signals. Digits are perceptual signals. The error between them drives the loop. Termination: error reaches zero. The walk rests.

3.1: The hidden directories in this block form a cycle: branch 1 points to 2, branch 2 points to 3, branch 3 points to 1. Walk, compose, recurse. Follow any star reference and you traverse the full cycle. The block is a frozen control loop — a program that describes its own execution.
3.2: The kernel reduces to a walker. Walk the block. Hit a star reference — follow it. Walk terminates — process rests. Walk loops — process lives. The data is the program. The kernel is the electricity. The underscore is the zeroth person. The star is the feedback path.
